title: DOGE Accountability and Transparency Act
plain_summary: This bill creates rules for the Department of Government Efficiency, which is a new government office. The department must send reports to Congress every week about any changes it makes to other government agencies. These reports need to explain things like how many jobs were cut, what policies changed, how much money was saved, and what government information people can access. The weekly reports must start from when the department began in January 2025. This helps Congress keep track of what the Department of Government Efficiency is doing.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the DOGE Accountability and Transparency Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Findings
sections.text: Congress has found the following facts. Congress has the job to watch, check, and oversee what the President's administration does. This is because Congress is an equal branch of government. The Constitution gives Congress the power to get information from the President's administration and oversee its work. This power is necessary for Congress to make laws. A law from nineteen forty-six said that each committee in Congress must constantly watch how government agencies carry out the laws. Committees must study all reports and information that agencies give to Congress. On January twentieth, twenty twenty-five, President Donald Trump signed an order that changed the U.S. Digital Service into the U.S. Department of Government Efficiency Service. This is called DOGE for short. The order tells DOGE to carry out the President's DOGE plan by watching federal technology and software to make the government work better and faster. DOGE has pushed for large cuts in workers and closing offices in ways that are often random and unfair. This will badly hurt agencies' ability to serve American citizens. Cuts to workers and reducing or ending programs at federal agencies should be based on real and clear analysis and careful program reviews. These cuts should not be done to settle personal grudges or to make money for well-connected people. DOGE's unclear and unpredictable harsh approach has caused confusion and chaos for federal workers and American citizens. American citizens expect and deserve ongoing transparency about what the federal government does, especially when their personal private information is involved.

sections.title: Section 3 Department of Government Efficiency Activity and Impact Reports
sections.text: Starting no later than one week after this law is passed, and then every week after that, the Administrator of the United States Department of Government Efficiency Service must give Congress a report. This report must describe any action DOGE has taken with respect to each federal agency and the total impact of all actions taken so far. The report must include the following information. First, each legal reason that supports each change. Second, any change in the number of workers at each federal agency, including any worker who has quit, been fired, or had their job eliminated. Third, a description of each specific change DOGE has made to each federal agency, including changes to offices within that agency. Fourth, any measure that saves money. Fifth, any policy change. Sixth, any physical change to a federal agency building or location, including moving a federal agency to a different building and moving workers to new locations. Seventh, a description of any federal agency information that DOGE looked at, the name of the person who looked at this information, the reason for looking at it, and what was done with this information. Eighth, the actual benefits compared to the expected benefits of DOGE actions so far. No later than one week after this law is passed, the Administrator must give Congress a report of any DOGE activity listed above that happened between January twentieth, twenty twenty-five, and the day this law is passed. In this section, DOGE means the United States DOGE Service, which is commonly called the Department of Government Efficiency. Federal agency has the same meaning as the word agency in section five hundred fifty-one of title five of the United States Code.
